title: Privacy & Recording Settings
lede: Decide exactly what your Smart Agent keeps from every conversation — full transcripts and audio recordings, or nothing at all. Two toggles give you total control over call data, so you can stay compliant without giving up the insights that make your agent smarter.

steps.title: Open Your Smart Agent
steps.content:
Privacy is configured per agent, so each agent can have its own recording policy — record your sales line, keep your medical-intake line storage-free.

  1. In the left sidebar, click Smart Agents (under Intelligence Center). You can also reach the same list from the top navigation via Automation → Smart Agents.
  2. In the agent list, click the name of the agent you want to configure. The agent editor opens.

images.alt: Smart Agents list with agent names in the first column
images.caption: Click an agent's name to open its editor.

images.alt: Smart Agents list with agent names in the first column
images.caption: Click an agent's name to open its editor.

steps.title: Open the Settings Panel
steps.content:
1. In the agent editor, look at the bottom of the large prompt card on the left.
  2. Click the Settings button (gear icon) — it sits next to Sub Agents / Tasks / Handoffs and Tools / MCP.
  3. The Settings window opens with a list of sections down the left side: General, Idle Messages, Voicemail, Speech, Voice Activity Detection, Audio Quality, Background Audio, Interruptions, User Turn Limits, Turn Detection, and Privacy & Recording.

images.alt: Agent editor with the Settings button at the bottom of the prompt card
images.caption: The Settings button lives at the bottom of the prompt card in the agent editor.

images.alt: Agent editor with the Settings button at the bottom of the prompt card
images.caption: The Settings button lives at the bottom of the prompt card in the agent editor.

steps.title: Select Privacy & Recording
steps.content:
1. In the Settings window, scroll the left rail to the bottom and click Privacy & Recording (lock icon, hint text "Data storage and audio recording").
  2. The right side now shows the Privacy & Recording card with two controls: Disable Sensitive Data Storage and Audio Recording.
💡 Changes here save instantly — there is no separate Save button. Click Done (or the ×) when you're finished; it simply closes the window.

images.alt: Settings window with Privacy & Recording selected in the left rail
images.caption: Privacy & Recording is the last section in the Settings rail.

images.alt: Settings window with Privacy & Recording selected in the left rail
images.caption: Privacy & Recording is the last section in the Settings rail.

steps.title: Understand the Two Controls
steps.content:
Here's what each pill toggle does:

  • Disable Sensitive Data Storage — the master privacy switch. When it shows ON, iMash will not store or log the call transcript or the call recording for this agent. When it shows OFF (the default), transcripts are saved and recording follows the toggle below.
  • Audio Recording — when ON (the default), the full conversation audio is recorded and attached to the call log so you can replay it later. Turn it OFF to keep transcripts but skip the audio file.

These settings apply to every call this agent handles — phone calls, web calls, and test calls alike.

images.alt: Privacy & Recording card with Disable Sensitive Data Storage OFF and Audio Recording ON
images.caption: The default state: storage enabled (toggle OFF) and Audio Recording ON.

images.alt: Privacy & Recording card with Disable Sensitive Data Storage OFF and Audio Recording ON
images.caption: The default state: storage enabled (toggle OFF) and Audio Recording ON.

steps.title: Go Fully Storage-Free (Optional)
steps.content:
Handling medical, legal, or payment conversations? Flip the master switch and iMash keeps nothing sensitive:

  1. Click the pill next to Disable Sensitive Data Storage so it reads ON.
  2. Notice that the Audio Recording row immediately greys out — recording is controlled by storage, so no audio is kept while storage is disabled, regardless of what the recording pill showed before.
⚠️ Trade-off to know before you flip it: with storage disabled there is no transcript to review in Call Logs, nothing for post-call analysis or summaries to work from, and no recording to settle "who said what" disputes. Only enable this when a regulation or policy truly requires it.

images.alt: Disable Sensitive Data Storage ON with the Audio Recording row greyed out
images.caption: With storage disabled, the Audio Recording control is locked — nothing is kept.

images.alt: Disable Sensitive Data Storage ON with the Audio Recording row greyed out
images.caption: With storage disabled, the Audio Recording control is locked — nothing is kept.

steps.title: Web-Call Video Recording (Node View)
steps.content:
If your agent takes web calls with video (for example through the website widget), there is a third control that only appears in the advanced editor:

  1. At the top of the agent editor, switch the Form / Node toggle to Node.
  2. Find the Privacy node on the canvas and click it. Its inspector shows the same two toggles plus Video Recording — "Record the user's video during a web call".
  3. The Privacy node's summary line tells you the current policy at a glance: Default recording + storage, audio off, video off, or PII redacted.

Like Audio Recording, Video Recording is automatically locked off while Disable Sensitive Data Storage is on.

steps.title: Tell Callers They're Being Recorded
steps.content:
Recording laws in many places require that callers are informed — some jurisdictions require all parties to consent. The easiest way to stay safe is to have your agent say so up front:

  1. Back in the agent editor (Form view), look at the top row of the prompt card. Set the first dropdown to Defined Message so the agent speaks first with your exact wording.
  2. In the message field next to it, include a short notice, for example: "Hi! This call may be recorded for quality and training purposes. How can I help you today?"
  3. For extra coverage, add a line to the system prompt instructing the agent to confirm recording status honestly if a caller asks whether the call is being recorded.
⚠️ Consent rules differ by country and state (one-party vs. all-party consent). This guide isn't legal advice — confirm your disclosure wording with your legal counsel, especially if you operate across borders.

images.alt: Defined Message selector and greeting field at the top of the agent editor
images.caption: Put your recording notice in the Defined Message greeting so it's spoken on every call.

images.alt: Defined Message selector and greeting field at the top of the agent editor
images.caption: Put your recording notice in the Defined Message greeting so it's spoken on every call.

steps.title: See What's Actually Stored in Call Logs
steps.content:
Trust, but verify — Call Logs is where every stored transcript and recording lives:

  1. In the left sidebar, click Call Logs (under Call Center).
  2. Use the AI Agent filter at the top to narrow the list to the agent you just configured.
  3. Each row is one call. This list (date, direction, duration, cost) is always kept for billing and reporting — the privacy toggles control the sensitive content of a call (transcript and audio), not the fact that a call happened.

images.alt: Call Logs page with filters and a list of recent calls
images.caption: Call Logs lists every call; the privacy toggles decide whether transcript and audio are attached.

images.alt: Call Logs page with filters and a list of recent calls
images.caption: Call Logs lists every call; the privacy toggles decide whether transcript and audio are attached.

steps.title: Open a Call to Confirm Your Policy Works
steps.content:
1. Click any call row to open the Call Details panel.
  2. With recording ON, an audio player appears at the top so you can replay the call, and the Transcripts tab shows the full conversation.
  3. With recording OFF (or storage disabled), the top banner reads "Recording not enabled for this call" — exactly what you want to see after locking things down.

That's it — your agent now keeps precisely as much (or as little) as your business and your regulators require. 🎉

images.alt: Call Details panel showing the banner Recording not enabled for this call
images.caption: The Call Details panel confirms your policy: no recording was kept for this call.

images.alt: Call Details panel showing the banner Recording not enabled for this call
images.caption: The Call Details panel confirms your policy: no recording was kept for this call.

steps.title: Compliance Quick Checklist
steps.content:
A short checklist to keep your call program clean:

  • Disclose recording in the agent's greeting wherever consent laws apply (see the step above).
  • Match the policy to the line: it's normal to record sales and support agents while running intake agents for regulated data (health, payments) storage-free.
  • Review before launch: place a test call, then check Call Logs to confirm the transcript/recording behavior matches your intent.
  • Limit who can listen: recordings and transcripts are visible to teammates with access to Call Logs — pair your privacy policy with sensible team permissions.
  • Audit changes: privacy toggles are configuration changes; keep an eye on your audit log so you know who changed a recording policy and when.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials

metaDescription: Control what your iMash Smart Agent stores: enable or disable call recording, go fully storage-free with Disable Sensitive Data Storage, add recording disclosures, and verify your policy in Call Logs.
metaKeywords: privacy settings, call recording, imash, smart agent, sensitive data storage, transcript, audio recording, video recording, compliance, consent
